violations.description: Preventing contamination of packaged and unpackaged food. — --Raw animal food was not properly separated from raw or cooked ready-to-eat food. - Raw hamburger patties stored above ready to eat foods in prep table.

Date marking and disposition of ready-to-eat potentially hazardous food. — --Refrigerated, ready-to-eat potentially hazardous food (prepared more than 24 hours ago) was not properly date marked. - Large tubs of raw chicken (in bags) thawing in reachin cooler not dated or labeled.

Design and construction of floors, walls, and ceilings. — --Floors, walls, and/or ceiling were not constructed or designed to be smooth, nonabsorbent, and easily cleanable. -Ceiling tiles in food prep area are acoustic. Needs to be smooth, nonabsorbent and, easily cleanable.
Preventing contamination during storage. — --Food item was not stored in a clean, dry location a minimum of 6 inches above the floor. Box of potatoes stored on floor in back storage area.

Preventing contamination of packaged and unpackaged food. — --Raw animal food was not properly separated from raw or cooked ready-to-eat food. Raw hamburger stored above cheese in back dining room refrigerator.

General equipment and utensils - Materials and design. — --Material of nonfood contact surface was not constructed or designed to be smooth, nonabsorbent and cleanable. Recommend sealing bare particle board shelving in back storage area.
Cleaning frequency of equipment and utensils. — --Nonfood contact surface was not cleaned as frequently as required. Cleaning needed around fryers.

Single-service and single-use articles. — --Single-service item was not stored, handled, dispensed in a manner to protect from contamination. Multiple boxes and sleeves of single service items stored on floor in back dining area and storage room.
Design, construction, clean, maintained (1 pt deducted)
Clean/dry location, protected, covered, original containers, labeled (2 pts deducted)
